content.body: A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers arranged in rows and columns. Matrices are used in computer graphics, solving systems of equations, economics, engineering, and coding theory.

A matrix with m rows and n columns is called an m×n matrix.

Example of a 2×3 matrix:
A = [1  2  3]
    [4  5  6]

Elements are denoted aᵢⱼ where i=row, j=column
a₁₂ = 2 (row 1, column 2)
a₂₃ = 6 (row 2, column 3)

Special matrices:
Square matrix: same number of rows and columns
Identity matrix I: square matrix with 1s on diagonal, 0s elsewhere
Zero matrix 0: all elements are 0

Matrix ADDITION and SUBTRACTION:
Only possible if matrices have the SAME order (same dimensions)
Add/subtract corresponding elements

[1 2] + [5 6] = [1+5  2+6] = [6  8]
[3 4]   [7 8]   [3+7  4+8]   [10 12]

Scalar multiplication (multiply by a number):
k × [a b] = [ka  kb]
    [c d]   [kc  kd]

questions.question: A 3×4 matrix has:
questions.explanation: An m×n matrix has m rows and n columns. 3×4 means 3 rows, 4 columns.

questions.question: Can you add a 2×3 matrix to a 3×2 matrix?
questions.explanation: Matrix addition requires identical dimensions. 2×3 and 3×2 are different shapes.

questions.question: The identity matrix I has:
options: 1s on the main diagonal and 0s elsewhere
questions.answer: 1s on the main diagonal and 0s elsewhere
questions.explanation: The identity matrix I has 1s along the main diagonal (top-left to bottom-right) and 0s everywhere else.

questions.question: For A(2×3) × B to be possible, B must have:
questions.explanation: Inner dimensions must match: A is 2×3, so B must start with 3 rows: B is 3×n.

questions.question: In matrix A, element a₂₁ refers to:
questions.explanation: aᵢⱼ: i is the row number, j is the column number. a₂₁ = row 2, column 1.

questions.question: A − A = ?
questions.explanation: Any matrix minus itself gives the zero matrix (all elements zero).

questions.question: Is matrix multiplication commutative? (AB = BA always?)
questions.explanation: Matrix multiplication is NOT commutative. AB ≠ BA in general (unlike regular number multiplication).

content.body: Probability measures how likely an event is to occur. It is expressed as a number between 0 and 1: 0 means impossible, 1 means certain. Probability is used in weather forecasting, insurance, games, medical testing, and many other fields.

Basic Probability:
P(event) = Number of favourable outcomes / Total number of possible outcomes

0 ≤ P(A) ≤ 1
P(impossible event) = 0
P(certain event) = 1
P(A) + P(A') = 1  →  P(A') = 1 − P(A)

A' is the complement of A (A does NOT happen)

content.title: Basic Probability Examples
content.body:
A bag has 3 red, 5 blue, 2 green balls (10 total)

P(red) = 3/10
P(blue) = 5/10 = 1/2
P(green) = 2/10 = 1/5
P(not red) = 1 − 3/10 = 7/10
P(red or blue) = (3+5)/10 = 8/10 = 4/5
P(yellow) = 0/10 = 0 (impossible)

Addition Rule:
P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) − P(A and B)

For MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE events (cannot both happen):
P(A and B) = 0
P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B)

Multiplication Rule:
For INDEPENDENT events (one doesn't affect the other):
P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B)

content.title: Combined Events
content.body:
Toss a coin AND roll a die:
P(Head) = 1/2, P(6) = 1/6
P(Head AND 6) = 1/2 × 1/6 = 1/12 (independent)

Draw 2 cards from a pack without replacement:
P(Ace then Ace) = 4/52 × 3/51 = 12/2652 = 1/221 (dependent!)

questions.question: A fair coin is tossed. P(Heads) = ?
questions.explanation: A fair coin has 2 equally likely outcomes. P(Heads) = 1/2.

questions.question: A fair die is rolled. P(getting a 4) = ?
questions.explanation: A die has 6 equally likely outcomes. P(4) = 1/6.

questions.question: P(A)=0.4, P(B)=0.3, mutually exclusive. P(A or B)=?
questions.explanation: Mutually exclusive: P(A or B) = P(A)+P(B) = 0.4+0.3 = 0.7.

questions.question: Two independent events: P(A)=0.5, P(B)=0.4. P(A and B)=?
Which probability is impossible?
questions.explanation: Probability must be between 0 and 1 inclusive. P=1.5 is not a valid probability.

questions.question: A card is drawn from 52. P(Ace or King)=?
questions.explanation: 4 Aces + 4 Kings = 8 cards. Mutually exclusive: P = 8/52 = 2/13.

questions.question: Roll a die twice. P(6 both times)=?
